---
title: "Homework 12"
format: html
---

# Question 1

```{python}
from math import exp
import json
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

gap = px.data.gapminder().query("year==1972")

europe = gap.query("continent == 'Europe'") #new

fig = px.choropleth(
    europe,
    locations = "iso_alpha",
    color = "gdpPercap",
    hover_name = "country",
    hover_data = {
        "gdpPercap": ":,.0f",
        "pop": ":,",
        "iso_alpha": False
    },
    color_continuous_scale = "Viridis",
    title = "GDP By Country in Europe (1972)" 
)

fig.update_geos(
    scope = "europe",
    showland = True,
    landcolor = "rgb(240,240,240)"
)   

fig.show()
```

## Something new
```{python}
fig = px.choropleth(
    europe,
    locations = "iso_alpha",
    color = "gdpPercap",
    hover_name = "country",
    hover_data = {
        "gdpPercap": ":,.0f",
        "pop": ":,",
        "iso_alpha": False
    },
    color_continuous_scale = "Viridis",
    range_color = (0, 5000),
    title = "GDP By Country in Europe (1972)" 
)

fig.update_geos(
    scope = "europe",
    showland = True,
    landcolor = "rgb(240,240,240)"
)   
fig.show()
```

## I used range_color to change the color range for GDP on the map
## The color range is now less variable, and doesn't show differences in GDP with color as well

# Question 2

```{python}
gap2 = px.data.gapminder().query("year==1972")

fig2 = px.scatter_geo(
    gap2,
    locations = "iso_alpha",
    size = "pop",
    color = "pop",
    hover_name = "country",
    hover_data ={
        "pop": ":,",
        "lifeExp": ":.1f"
    },
    #color_continuous_scale = "Viridis",
    title = "Country Population Sizes (1972)"

)
fig2.show()
```

## something new

```{python}
fig2.update_traces(opacity=0.5)
fig2.show()
```

## The new argument I used was update_traces to change the opacity of the map colors
## It changed the map by making the colors of the bubbles on the map less opaque
